name: Warsaw (Mazowieckie, Poland)

colors.symbolism: the lower stripe and traditional Polish colour

description: Two horizontal stripes of equal width — yellow over red — the colours derived from the city's coat of arms (the Warsaw Mermaid, Syrenka).
history: Warsaw is the capital and largest city of Poland. Its plain bicolour flag was adopted in 1938. The colours come from the city's historic coat of arms.

features.symbolism: the heraldic colours of Warsaw — gold and red — taken from the city's coat of arms (the Mermaid of Warsaw on red, with gold details)
